prompts.prompt: Show me the most important events tracked in my Mixpanel project over the last 7 days and their trends.

prompts.title: Retention insights
prompts.prompt: Give me insights about user retention and cohort behavior over the past month.

SETUP_GUIDE:
# Mixpanel Integration Setup

## Step 1: Get Your Project Token
1. Log in to your Mixpanel account at https://mixpanel.com
2. Navigate to your project settings
3. Under 'Project Settings', find your **Project Token**
4. Copy the token and add it as `MIXPANEL_PROJECT_TOKEN` in your .env file

## Step 2: Get Your API Secret
1. In Mixpanel, go to Settings > Project Settings
2. Scroll to 'Service Accounts' section
3. Create a new Service Account or use an existing one
4. Copy the **API Secret** and add it as `MIXPANEL_API_SECRET` in your .env file

## Step 3: Get Your Project ID
1. In Mixpanel project settings URL, your Project ID is the number in the URL
2. Format: https://mixpanel.com/project/YOUR_PROJECT_ID/settings
3. Copy this ID and add it as `MIXPANEL_PROJECT_ID` in your .env file

## Step 4: Set Up Environment Variables
Add these to your `.env` file:
```
MIXPANEL_PROJECT_TOKEN=your_project_token_here
MIXPANEL_API_SECRET=your_api_secret_here
MIXPANEL_PROJECT_ID=your_project_id_here
```

## Step 5: Test the Integration
Run your application and try tracking a test event to verify the setup is working correctly.

## Important Notes
- **Project Token** is used for tracking events (ingestion)
- **API Secret** is used for querying and exporting data
- Keep both credentials secure and never commit them to version control
- For production deployments, use environment variables or secure secret management

## Useful Resources
- [Mixpanel API Documentation](https://developer.mixpanel.com/reference/overview)
- [Event Tracking Guide](https://docs.mixpanel.com/docs/tracking-methods/sdks)
- [Query API Guide](https://developer.mixpanel.com/reference/query-api)
